title: Chain-Drive Motorised Hacksaw Reciprocating Mechanism
design_question: How to convert rotary motor output to a guided linear reciprocating hacksaw stroke via chain drive?
selected_approach: 750 W induction motor drives a #40 roller chain sprocket (12T:48T, ratio 4:1). Large sprocket (48T, 150 mm PCD) carries an offset crank pin (r=40 mm, stroke 80 mm). Connecting rod links crank pin to hacksaw frame guided in linear slideways. Cut/return ratio: standard slider-crank 1:1 (simple circle), or quick-return offset geometry for 1.4:1 ratio.
rationale: Chain drive handles the shock loads of cutting better than belt drive (no slip). Slider-crank provides smooth sinusoidal velocity profile. 80 mm stroke suited for 300 mm hacksaw blade. Motor 750 W adequate for 50 mm mild steel at 80 strokes/min.

alternatives.approach: Direct DC motor with ball screw
alternatives.reason_rejected: DC motor inefficient for reciprocating duty.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Slider-Crank to Reciprocating Hacksaw Drive' confirms stroke S = 2r and power formula.
supporting_fact_titles: DC Gear Motor with PWM Speed Control
Power Transmission — Chain Drive and Sprocket
